mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and equity risk premia, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month when the easing trend is persistent rather than temporary.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative or indistinguishable from zero in periods where FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above its recent baseline forces tighter policy expectations and multiple compression, which pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3pp over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration trigger episodes do not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases across a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth and UNRATE improvement occur together, the labor market is confirming expanding private demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if joint labor-improvement episodes fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either PAYEMS alone or UNRATE alone fully explains the effect with no incremental interaction gain.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue support, which should lift equity earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if a sustained increase in RSAFS is followed by negative or zero 20-day SPX returns in the majority of cases, or if the sign flips in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO with flat or falling HOUST signals improving industrial capacity without a broad overheating shock, which is consistent with positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1% in 2 months while HOUST does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the combined industrial-strength condition does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect disappears when INDPRO is considered without HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment spike signals deteriorating household income and weaker earnings demand, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next week as investors reprice recession risk quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike episodes fail to produce negative 5-day SPX returns or if positive returns dominate despite clear labor deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing together with weak retail sales signals a synchronized domestic demand slowdown, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% and RSAFS growth turns negative over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-retail contraction does not predict weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the outcome without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening phase, stable inflation with a still-elevated but not rising FEDFUNDS rate supports valuation expansion after policy uncertainty fades, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if, in post-tightening pauses, 60-day SPX returns are not above baseline or if stable CPIAUCSL combined with flat FEDFUNDS fails to outperform all other regimes.
